data.title: Today's Market: S&P 500 Flat, Nasdaq Dips as Robust Home Sales Ease Recession Fears
data.description:
The S&P 500 hovered near flat on Thursday as investors struggled to build on previous gains, while the Nasdaq Composite fell about 0.2% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average inched up 91 points, or roughly 0.2%.

New economic data suggests recession fears may be overblown. The National Association of Realtors reported that February sales of previously owned homes rose 4.2% from January, defying expectations of a 3% drop. Jobless claims increased slightly, and layoffs remained low, easing some market concerns.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell described the economy as strong overall and indicated that the impact of tariffs on inflation is likely to be short-lived. Analyst Sam Stovall, CFRA Research's chief investment strategist, commented, Bull markets don't die of old age. They die of fright. He added that many investors expect a rate cut in June.

Following Wednesday's Fed meeting, which forecast two rate cuts this year while holding the federal funds rate at 4.25% to 4.5%, traders remain cautious amid ongoing tariff policy uncertainty.

Canada Economics Brief: Macklem Says Canada "Somewhat Squeezed" By Tariff Disputes With U.S. and With China, Adds "No Question" That Is Having An Impact On Economy Here
Update: WTI Oil Closes Higher as the U.S. Places Fresh Sanctions on Iran
data.description:
West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil rose on Thursday following a day-prior report showing U.S. oil inventories rose last month but gasoline inventories dropped, while the United States placed additional sanctions on Iran.

WTI crude for April delivery closed up US$1.10 to settle at US$68.26 per barrel, while May Brent crude was last seen up US$1.15 to US$71.93.

Reuters reported the United States tightened sanctions on Iran's oil sector, including a sanction on a Chinese independent refinery and 12 other entities.

The Energy Information Administration on Wednesday reported U.S. oil inventories rose by 1.7-million barrels last week, while gasoline stocks fell by 0.5-million barrels.

"Although crude stock increased by 1.7mb, they are 8mb down on the year and some 19mb below the 5-year average. But what instilled buying interest came from Gasoline stocks now being at their lowest this year as we approach the hallowed driving season of the summer," PVM Oil Associates said in a note.

Israel's decision to break a ceasefire agreement with the Hamas militant group and U.S. attacks on Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi militia continue keep traders attention on potential threats to Persian Gulf supply, while Russia and Ukraine continue their war even as the United States continues to attempt to force a ceasefire on the combatants.

The Federal Reserve's policy committee ended its meeting on Wednesday leaving interest rates unchanged, even as it warned President Donald Trump's tariff wars are likely to boost inflation and slow growth.

Five Below's Turnaround Seen as Promising — Market Talk
data.description: Five Below is taking the necessary measures to turn around its business, and the process looks promising in the early stages, UBS analysts say in a research note. The discount retailer has already seen green shots from efforts to provide more newness and improve the shopping experience. There are several catalysts that could drive the stock higher, such as any rollback of tariffs and the potential for consumers to trade down. Still, UBS cuts its target price to $110 from $150. Five Below is up 3.6% higher at $78.40.(sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Williams-Sonoma Operating Margin Outlook 'Disappointing' Due to Tariff Headwinds, Wedbush Says
data.description:
Williams-Sonoma had strong fiscal Q4 results, but its operating margin outlook was "disappointing" because of tariff headwinds, Wedbush said in a note Thursday.

The company's fiscal Q4 revenue was better than expected as it recognized improving furniture trends, and its gross margins of 47.3% improved 130 basis points year over year, Wedbush analysts said.

The company's operating margin guidance came in lower than expected at 17.4% to 17.8%, compared with the consensus of 18.1%, and reflected the higher costs from all currently announced tariffs.

The analysts noted that although Williams-Sonoma acknowledged that its fiscal Q1 was not trending as strongly as the previous quarter, the company did not copy other retailers in announcing a broad-based slowdown for the period.

Wedbush lowered its target price for the company to $170 from $190 and reiterated the rating at neutral.

data.title: PDD Holdings Is Maintained at Buy by Jefferies
EU wheat eases from two-week top, rapeseed extends rebound
data.description:
Euronext wheat edged lower on Thursday after notching a two-week high as traders assessed export demand and negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.

Front-month May milling wheat (BL2K5), the most active position on Euronext, settled 0.2% down at 226.75 euros ($245.84) per metric ton.

The contract earlier extended gains from Wednesday to reach its highest level since March 3 at 228.25 euros.

Euronext drew support from the euro (EUR=-), which fell further back from a five-month high against the dollar, and news on Wednesday that Turkey is loosening curbs on wheat imports.

But a fall in Chicago, where traders reacted to weak old-crop export sales, encouraged Euronext to consolidate.

There was also caution before U.S.-Russian talks on Monday that are due to cover shipping security in the Black Sea, as part of efforts to secure a ceasefire in Russia's war with Ukraine.

"Continued global geopolitical risks will likely weigh on the market, with the Russia-Ukraine ceasefire negotiations ... all against an equally volatile backdrop of trade tensions," British merchant ADM Agriculture Ltd said in a note.

Reaction to Turkey's relaxation of its import restrictions was tempered by a sharp drop in the country's currency after the arrest of the main opposition leader.

"This means you have to expect that Turkish mills will delay purchases at least for the next few weeks in the hope that Turkey's currency will stabilise," one German trader said.

Western European wheat was also seen facing continuing competition from Black Sea suppliers, despite a slowdown in Russian exports.

Traders said Russian 12.5% protein wheat on Thursday was priced around $251-$254 a ton FOB for April/May shipment and Romanian around $254-$256.

Russian 11.5% wheat was around $244-$246, Ukrainian wheat also was quoted at about these levels, with Romanian about $5 a ton higher.

French and other West European Union 11.5% wheat was priced around $7-$8 a ton higher than Russian/Ukrainian depending on Euronext levels, another trader said.

In France, Morocco remained the main destination for wheat exports outside the EU, with six vessels due to load this week, according to LSEG data. (GRAIN/SHP/FR)

In rapeseed, May futures (COMK5) on Euronext settled 1.2% up at 492.00 euros per ton.

The contract was extending a technical rebound from a six-month low earlier this week fueled by tariff concerns over Canadian rapeseed, dealers said.

($1 = 0.9223 euros)

data.title: DATA BREACH ALERT: Edelson Lechtzin LLP Is Investigating Claims On Behalf Of Pennsylvania State Education Association (PSEA) Members Whose Data May Have Been Compromised
data.description:
NEWTOWN, Pa., March 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Edelson Lechtzin LLP, a national class action law firm, is investigating data privacy violations involving the Pennsylvania State Education Association (“PSEA”). PSEA learned of suspicious activity on its network on or about July 6, 2024. To join this case, go HERE.

What is the Pennsylvania State Education Association?

The Pennsylvania State Education Association (PSEA) is a labor organization representing over 177,000 educators, support staff, and retirees.

What happened?

On or around July 6, 2024, PSEA experienced a data breach that affected more than 500,000 individuals. A comprehensive investigation and review of the impacted data concluded on February 18, 2025. This investigation determined that an unauthorized party accessed specific files within PSEA’s network, some of which contained sensitive personal information.

What type of information was stolen?

The personal information in the compromised files may have included:

  • Driver’s License Numbers/State ID’s
  • Social Security Numbers
  • Banking Information
  • Passport Numbers
  • Medical Information

How can I protect my personal data?

If you receive a data breach notification concerning PSEA, you must guard against possible misuse of your personal information, including identity theft and fraud, by regularly reviewing your account statements and monitoring your credit reports for suspicious or unauthorized activity.

Edelson Lechtzin LLP is investigating a class action lawsuit to seek legal remedies for individuals whose sensitive personal data may have been compromised by the PSEA data breach.

For more information, please contact:

Marc H. Edelson, Esq.

EDELSON LECHTZIN LLP

411 S. State Street, Suite N-300

Newtown, PA 18940

Phone: 844-696-7492 ext. 2

Email: medelson@edelson-law.com

Web: www.edelson-law.com  

About Edelson Lechtzin LLP

Edelson Lechtzin LLP is a national class action law firm with offices in Pennsylvania and California. In addition to cases involving data breaches, our lawyers focus on class and collective litigation in cases alleging securities and investment fraud, violations of the federal antitrust laws, employee benefit plans under ERISA, wage theft and unpaid overtime, consumer fraud, and catastrophic injuries.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions.

data.title: Front-Month Gold Sets Another Record in 8-Session Wining Streak — Market Talk
data.description: Front-month gold finishes up 0.1% to $3,040 a troy ounce, a new record--marking the eighth straight session that front-month gold futures settled higher, climbing 5% over that span. Traders remain risk adverse, says Alex Ebkarian of Allegiance Gold. "We continue to see investors gravitating toward safe-haven gold, moving away from riskier investments such as stocks and cryptocurrencies," says Ebkarian. Numerous other economic factors are fueling uncertainty that's making investors uneasy, he adds. SPDR Gold shares are down 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Washington Trust Bancorp Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.56 per Share, Payable April 11 to Holders of Record April 1
Canadians Trade Tips to Boycott American Stuff. Great App, Eh? — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

It takes a lot to offend a Canadian. The U.S. has finally crossed that line.

It's not just President Donald Trump's tariffs on Canadian products that are angering them. It's his repeated calls for Canada to become the "51st state," and his name-calling of former Prime Minister Justin Trudeau as "Governor," and perhaps his description of Canada as "one of the nastiest countries to deal with."

Canadians are vowing to spend their Canadian dollars at home, and are sharing tips on social media about how to boycott U.S. brands and companies. There are even apps for that.

Take O SCANada, an app developed by Calgary resident Ryan Checora and his mother, Cathy, in response to the emerging trade war. "We found that the question 'Is it Canadian?' rarely has a straightforward answer — unless you're talking about maple syrup!" he said.

Shoppers can scan a barcode with the AI-enabled app, and it will break down the product's origin according to its "brand authenticity and contribution to the Canadian economy," he told Barron's. The app, which has been downloaded more than 80,000 times, also suggests Canadian products as alternatives to U.S. brands.

"So far responses have been very positive!" Checora said in an email. "We certainly did not expect the app to gain so much popularity the way that it has."

Likewise, AI-enabled Maple Scan app helps Canadians shop locally, support their economy, and better understand the origin of their everyday items, according to Calgary developer Sasha Ivanov, who created it about a month ago. More than 100,000 people have downloaded it and used it to scan more than 320,000 items, pushing it to the fifth spot on the Canada iOS App Store, Ivanov said.

Ivanov says the fellow Canadians he's talked to since launching the app have unanimously said they have permanently changed their buying habits, "regardless of future potential changes to trading relations between the U.S. and Canada." He has also gotten encouragement from people and businesses in Europe, Asia, South America, Mexico, and even the U.S.

"All in all, this has been a challenging time for Canadians, but I feel we are also united in the face of these changes," Ivanov told Barron's.

Canadians are adding Canadian flags and maple leaves to their social media profiles and tagging their posts with hashtags like #ElbowsUp, a reference to a defensive hockey move that has become a nationwide rallying cry for Canadian unity and pride.

Grocery stores in Canada have affixed maple leaf tags to their shelves to signal Canadian goods.

"Canadian shoppers are starting a new habit," one person posted on social media. "After checking an item, if it is a US product they return it to the shelf upside down and turn several more of the same item upside down so the next shopper knows to skip that product."

"When things get tough Canadians get tougher," another person posted on Bluesky. "Buy Local, Buy Canadian, Cancel, Boycott everything American!"

Trump enacted 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico on March 4, and while he quickly delayed implementing many of them for a month, especially for autos, he has promised more to come in April. There are also Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to the U.S., where Canada is a significant supplier.

When asked why he was being tougher on Canada than some of America's biggest adversaries, Trump told Fox News: "Only because it's meant to be our 51st state."

Prime Minister Mark Carney, who has dismissed such talk as disrespectful and said Canada will never be part of the U.S., said on X on Thursday that they're ready to engage with the U.S. on trade "when Canada is shown respect as a sovereign nation."

Peace by Chocolate, a chocolate company in Antigonish, Nova Scotia, founded by a Syrian family that moved to Canada after their chocolate factory in Damascus was bombed in 2012, said it has sold thousands of its $6.25 "Elbows Up" chocolate bars since launching them on March 9.

"Peace is something you defend. It's something you build and protect. That's what 'Elbows Up' means for us at Peace by Chocolate — in standing firm against forces that try to shake us," founder and CEO Tareq Hadhad told Barron's, adding that orders for the chocolate bars are coming in from at least 15 countries.

Gary Innis, the general manager of Urban Grocer, a neighborhood market in Victoria, British Columbia, told local news station Chek Media that customers are picking Canadian produce over lower-priced U.S. brands like Earthbound Farms organic lettuce, and that he recently rejected a truckload of oranges from the U.S.

"We literally can't give away American product right now," assistant produce manager Rob Avery said.

Canadians are also canceling travel plans to the U.S. Flight Centre Travel Group Canada reported a 40% decrease in leisure bookings to the U.S. last month compared with February 2024, and spokesperson Amra Durakovic told Barron's that Canadians instead are choosing Mexico, Jamaica, the Dominican Republic, and Caribbean cruises and European destinations such as Amsterdam, France, Portugal, Italy, and the U.K.

The U.S. Travel Association has said that the 20.4 million Canadians who visited the U.S. last year generated $20.5 billion in spending and supported 140,000 American jobs. "A 10% reduction in Canadian travel could mean 2.0 million fewer visits, $2.1 billion in lost spending, and 14,000 job losses."

Consumers aren't the only ones boycotting. Toronto Mayor Olivia Chow said Monday that the city will buy its next 17 fire trucks from Canadian companies instead of U.S. manufacturers, and that U.S.-based suppliers are no longer welcome to bid on city contracts.

"When you impose a senseless and hurtful trade war, especially in Toronto, we're going to fight back," Chow told CP24 News. "We're gonna keep our elbows up to Trump to say 'No way, not a chance, no 51st state.'"

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: INNOVATIVE DESIGNS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Innovative Designs Inc., a company specializing in advanced insulation materials, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing substantial improvements in both financial performance and operational efficiency. The report highlights the company's successful strategies in increasing sales, particularly in its House Wrap product line, and its ongoing efforts to enhance production capabilities and quality control.

Financial Highlights

Revenues, net: $543,916, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year's $65,886, primarily due to higher sales of the House Wrap product line.

Income (loss) from operations: $40,787, compared to a loss of $(54,620) in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Net income (loss): $36,202, a substantial improvement from a net loss of $(63,393) in the previous year, driven by increased sales and better cost management.

Loss per share of common stock - Basic and diluted: $0.000, compared to $(0.002) in the prior year, reflecting the company's return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The increase in revenue for the three-month period ended January 31, 2025, is attributed solely to an increase in sales of the House Wrap product line, which is made from IINSULTEX material.

Operational Performance: The company has been focusing on increasing sales through hiring a consultant, specifically the son of the former CEO, to boost sales efforts.

Production and Equipment: The company has made significant deposits on equipment to produce the Insultex material under its own brand name. The equipment delivery and installation are pending, with plans to ensure compliance with environmental regulations.

Quality Control and Certification: New quality control testing equipment for the House Wrap product line has been built, with deposits paid. The company is working towards ICC-ES certification, which is expected to facilitate larger orders for the House Wrap product.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue funding operations through sales, borrowings, and potential securities sales. The focus remains on operationalizing the Insultex production equipment and securing necessary certifications to expand market reach.

SEC Filing: INNOVATIVE DESIGNS INC [ IVDN ] - 10-Q - Mar. 20, 2025

data.description: Accenture has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $398.67, according to analysts polled by FactSet.
